sections.about: This metric measures energy consumed per tonne of crude steel or physical output under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator 1. It is the most relevant energy-efficiency metric in this set because it adjusts for scale. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Jindal Stainless leads in every comparable year, improving from 19.97 GJ/tcs in FY2024 to 16.36 GJ/tcs in FY2025. JSW is second in FY2025, improving to 23.547 GJ/tcs. SAIL improved from 32 GJ/tcs in FY2024 to 27 GJ/tcs in FY2025. Tata reported 23.43 GJ/tcs in FY2023 (standalone, steelmaking sites only), then worsened to 28.3 GJ/tcs in FY2024 and stayed at 28.3 GJ/tcs in FY2025. Tata’s BRSR energy-efficiency initiatives, including BEE collaboration, waste heat recovery and process gas recovery, are directionally positive, but they have not yet translated into peer-leading intensity. Jindal’s EAF liquid chrome optimisation, VFDs, waste heat recovery boiler and hot charging initiatives directly target electricity and fuel intensity.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata ranks 4th, with energy intensity 73.0% above Jindal Stainless. Tata’s rank was 2nd in FY2023 at 23.43 GJ/tcs, declined to 3rd in FY2024 as intensity worsened to 28.3 GJ/tcs, and dropped further to 4th in FY2025 as SAIL improved to 27 GJ/tcs. This is the most concerning Tata energy metric because it removes the large producer explanation and points to a direct efficiency gap.
sections.targets: Tata discloses energy-efficiency programmes across geographies and BEE collaboration. SAIL has a phased decarbonisation and PAT-linked efficiency roadmap. Jindal Stainless has PAT overachievement at Jajpur and Hisar and disclosed multiple FY2025 energy-saving initiatives.
sections.comparabilityNotes: SAIL FY2022-23 data is not available. Tata FY2022-23 intensity (23.43 GJ/tcs) is a standalone figure covering steelmaking sites only, which limits direct comparability with peers that may report on a broader operational boundary.

flags.flagNote: SAIL FY2023 physical-output intensity is missing, so the FY2023-24 SAIL trend cannot be assessed.
